div.h1: Mutex vs. Spinlock Decision: Execution Context Matrix
div.p: Y-Axis: Can the current execution context sleep? X-Axis: Does the critical section contain operations that may sleep?

ul.li: ✅ YELLOW-GREEN: Correct, choose based on contention profile
✅ YELLOW: Safe for atomic contexts only

🚫 RED: ILLEGAL — triggers kernel BUG() / panic M4 Driver Analysis: All file_operations handlers run in process (syscall) context → execution context CAN sleep → top row → mutex is always correct. mydevice_read() and mydevice_write() call copy_to/from_user() (may fault/sleep) and wait_event_interruptible() (explicitly sleeps) → Q1: MUTEX required.

text.tspan: • IRQ handler: flip a status bit
